channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Here’s your last-minute guide to holiday social media etiquette
item.description: There are certain social media rules we can all agree on: Ghosting a conversation is impolite, and replying “k” to a text is the equivalent of a backhand slap (violent, wrong, and rude).
item.dc:creator: Steffi Cao, Fast Company

item.title: TikTok rolling out age restrictions on beauty filters amid mental health concerns
item.description: TikTok announced Tuesday it will restrict the use of some appearance effects for users under 18 after teens and parents in a new study expressed concerns about the impact of beauty filters.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Northern lights may be faintly visible across parts of the US this Thanksgiving
item.description: Solar storms may produce faint auroras across the northern rim of the United States this Thanksgiving.
item.dc:creator: Adithi Ramakrishnan, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Australia's social media ban for kids is closer to becoming law
item.description: MELBOURNE, Australia (AP) — Australia’s House of Representatives on Wednesday passed a bill that would ban children younger than 16 years old from social media, leaving it to the Senate to finalize the world-first law.
item.dc:creator: ROD McGUIRK, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taking stock of human capital in the age of AI
item.description: Turnover has always been a concern for employers, but in a recent PwC report, an increasing number of employees worldwide are looking to move within the next year in light of many things, but mostly because of opportunities afforded by the rapid pace of technological change.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Australian father of teen sextortion victim backs banning young children from social media
item.description: Wayne Holdsworth became an advocate for banning Australian children younger than 16 from social media because his son took his own life after falling victim to an online sextortion scam.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Federal prosecutors seek records from company that deployed AI weapons scanner on NYC subway
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Federal investigators in New York are seeking records from the manufacturer of an AI-powered weapons scanner that was briefly deployed this summer in New York City’s subway system.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Cisco, NTT Data partner to simplify private 5G connectivity
item.description: Cisco and NTT Data are extending their partnership to offer customers more streamlined options for deploying private 5G services for enterprise connectivity. The partnership ties Cisco’s networking technologies to NTT Data’s Transatel network.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: These digital product trends will define 2025
item.description: Consumer preferences, technology innovations, and emerging market forces—from AI capabilities to platform shifts and sustainability imperatives—shape how we build digital products and their success in the market.
item.dc:creator: Brad Weber, Fast Company

item.title: Anthropic introduces the Model Context Protocol
item.description: Anthropic today released a new open source protocol to let all AI systems, not just its own, connect with data sources via a standard interface.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Interpol clamps down on cybercrime and arrests over 1,000 suspects in Africa
item.description: Interpol says it arrested 1,006 suspects in Africa during a massive two-month operation, clamping down on cybercrime that left tens of thousands of victims, including some who were trafficked, and produced millions in financial damages.
item.dc:creator: Mark Banchereau, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Google, DOJ wrap up second antitrust trial
item.description: The Department of Justice (DOJ) and Google wrapped up the tech giant’s second antitrust trial in two years Monday, as the company seeks to fend off monopoly allegations in the advertising technology space. After a relatively brief two-week trial in September, the two sides reconvened in U.S.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: PHP updates DOM API
item.description: PHP 8.4.1, an update to the longstanding server-side web scripting language, has been released with features including an updated DOM API that makes it easier to work with HTML5 documents. Accessible from php.net, PHP 8.4.1 was released November 21.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: North Korean hackers use disguised apps to target Macs with hidden malware
item.description: Tech expert Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson says North Korean hackers hide malware in macOS apps, bypassing Mac security checks.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Intel’s CHIPS Act grant reduced as production delays and losses mount
item.description: The US government has scaled back Intel’s preliminary CHIPS Act grant from $8.5 billion to under $8 billion, reflecting concerns over the company’s delayed investments and financial woes, The New York Times reported.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How to use iPhone's mic mode for crystal-clear, noise-free calls
item.description: Kurt “CyberGuy" Knutsson reveals how iPhone’s new Automatic Mic Mode adjusts settings for optimal call quality automatically.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Speed is the killer app
item.description: Why do enterprises so often choose to run applications in the cloud even when it may not be the cheapest option? Why do they turn to open source even if it’s not the most feature-complete choice? And why is generative AI so frothy hot even though, as my InfoWorld colleague David Linthicum argues, “C
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: 3 data engineering trends riding Kafka, Flink, and Iceberg
item.description: Apache Kafka, Apache Flink, and Apache Iceberg are among the most popular technologies in the data ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Bitcoin ticks closer to $100,000 in extended surge following US elections
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Bitcoin extended its streak of record highs after ticking above $99,000 for the first time. The cryptocurrency has rocketed more than 40% in just two weeks.
item.dc:creator: WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Risk and Compliance Exchange 2024: DoD’ Stacy Bostjanick, DCMA’s Jennifer Henderson on finding ‘any means possible’ to help small biz with CMMC
item.description: As the Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification program inches closer to reality, DoD prepares defense industrial base for compliance requirements.
item.dc:creator: Justin Doubleday, Federal News Network

item.title: Supreme Court allows multibillion-dollar class action to proceed against Meta
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Supreme Court is allowing a multibillion-dollar class action investors’ lawsuit to proceed against Facebook parent Meta, stemming from the privacy scandal involving the Cambridge Analytica political consulting firm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Thai court dismisses activist's suit against Israeli spyware producer over lack of evidence
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — A Thai court on Thursday threw out a lawsuit brought by a pro-democracy activist which alleged spyware produced by an Israeli tech firm had been used to hack his phone.
item.dc:creator: JERRY HARMER and DAVID RISING, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nile unwraps NaaS security features for enterprise customers
item.description: Network-as-a-service startup Nile has added new zero-trust capabilities and tapped Microsoft and Palo Alto Networks to bolster its enterprise security services. Nile was founded by former Cisco CEO John Chambers and Pankaj Patel, Cisco’s former chief development officer.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: SONiC pioneer Aviz Networks raises $17M for AI network management push
item.description: Networking software provider Aviz Networks today announced a $17 million Series A funding round to accelerate its growth in open networking solutions and artificial intelligence capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Nvidia is Wall Street's most valuable company. How it got there, by the numbers
item.description: Nvidia has once again turned out quarterly results that exceeded Wall Street's forecasts. The company has seen soaring demand for its semiconductors, which are used to power artificial intelligence applications.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Comcast to spin off cable networks that were once the entertainment giant's star performers
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Comcast is spinning off into a new company many of the cable television networks that were once at the entertainment giant's heart as consumers increasingly swap out their cable TV subscriptions for streaming platforms.
item.dc:creator: MATT OTT and WYATTE GRANTHAM-PHILIPS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
